given: No stock records exist in the database
when: User navigates to the stock home page
then: An explicit empty state message is displayed guiding the user that no stock has been recorded yet, instead of showing an empty or blank table
architectural_notes: Explicit empty-state handling owned by the boundary/template (app/routes/): when the service returns an empty list from the repository, the view renders a guidance message instead of a blank table. E2E: asserts the message against an empty DB. Cross-cutting: explicit-state design (no silent blank region); the empty-vs-populated branch is a view decision, the empty result itself comes from the repository read.

independence.rationale: Handles the absence-of-data scenario separately from AC1-AC4, which assume records exist; the empty state is a distinct observable outcome required by the feature's design principle of explicit states.
